channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Impeached South Korean president issues defiant message as he faces possible detention
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol, in a message to conservative supporters rallying outside his Seoul residence, vowed to “fight to the end” against “anti-state forces,” while his legal team warned Thursday that police officers attempting to detain him could fa
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance shares Musk op-ed backing Germany's far-right party
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance has shared Elon Musk's op-ed supporting the far-right German political party Alternative for Germany (AfD), while the German government has accused Musk of trying to influence the upcoming February election.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: Israeli airstrike hits a Gaza humanitarian zone as Netanyahu OKs a delegation to talks in Qatar
item.description: Israeli airstrikes across Gaza have killed at least 40 people, including several children, as they struck Hamas security officers and an Israeli-declared humanitarian zone.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk urges release of far-right UK figure; blasts PM Starmer
item.description: Elon Musk has called for the release of far-right activist Tommy Robinson and criticized British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on social media, while also praising Nigel Farage's Reform UK party and the AfD party in Germany.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: New Zealand police officer killed in New Year's Day vehicle attack, another injured
item.description: A driver ran down two New Zealand police officers on patrol in the early hours of New Year’s Day, killing one and seriously injuring the other, the country’s police chief said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Puerto Rico's new governor sworn in days after a major blackout left much of the island in the dark
item.description: Jenniffer González Colón has been sworn in as Puerto Rico’s new governor as the island held for a normally ebullient ceremony overshadowed by widespread anger over a blackout that hit the U.S. territory days ago.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bangladesh court again rejects bail for Hindu leader who led rallies
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — A court in southeastern Bangladesh on Thursday rejected a plea for bail by a jailed Hindu leader who led large rallies in the Muslim-majority country demanding better security for minority groups.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: British rapper Stormzy banned from driving for using phone behind wheel of his Rolls-Royce
item.description: British rapper Stormzy has been banned from driving for nine months after admitting that he drove his Rolls-Royce through London while using his phone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Montenegro mourns after gunman kills at least 12 people before shooting himself
item.description: Shock and dismay prevails in Montenegro after a gunman fatally shot 12 people, including two children, in a western town before killing himself.
item.dc:creator: Predrag Milic, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, WGN 9

item.title: Protesters in Syria demand justice for disappeared activists and accountability from all factions
item.description: DOUMA, Syria (AP) — Protesters in Syria held a sit-in Wednesday demanding justice for four activists who were forcibly disappeared in 2013 and whose fate remains one of the most haunting mysteries of the country's 13-year civil war. On Dec.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Gaza, including children, as war grinds into the new year
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes killed at least 12 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip, mostly women and children, officials said Wednesday, as the nearly 15-month war ground on into the new year.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — From Sydney to Mumbai to Paris to Rio de Janeiro, communities around the world welcomed 2025 with spectacular light shows, embraces and ice plunges.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, CEDAR ATTANASIO and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An armed man kills at least 10 people, including 2 children, in a shooting rampage in Montenegro
item.description: Montenegro’s interior minister says at least 10 people, including two children, were killed in a shooting in the western city of Cetinje.
item.dc:creator: Predrag Milic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: South Korea air crash investigators extract black box data as grieving families mourn the victims
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Grieving relatives of the victims of the South Korea plane crash gathered at the site to pay respects to their loved ones on New Year’s Day, as officials said they've extracted data from one of the retrieved black boxes to find the exact cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Dozens of patients and wounded evacuated from Gaza for treatment
item.description: Dozens of patients and the wounded have been evacuated for treatment outside the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, where the United Nations says Israel's attacks on and around hospitals have pushed health care to the brink.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel intercepts Houthi missile fired from Yemen
item.description: Israel’s military said it intercepted a missile fired toward the country by Yemen’s Houthi rebels, setting off sirens late Monday in central areas including Tel Aviv.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin offers to pay off debts as recruitment tool in war against Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a bill into law last month that offers to pay off up to $96,000 in debt in exchange for military service.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of soldiers freed in the latest prisoner exchange between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine have exchanged prisoners of war in the latest such swap that saw the release of hundreds of captives and was brokered with the help of the United Arab Emirates, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Uncovering the atrocities of the Assad regime at its 'death factory' on the hill
item.description: Sednaya prison was the site where thousands of Syrians were tortured and killed under Assad’s regime. Investigators have uncovered mass graves, and are calling for justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea deadly plane crash: US sends investigators to country still reeling from disaster that killed 179
item.description: The U.S. is sending investigators to South Korea to probe the deadly plane crash that killed 179 people as officials comb through over 600 body parts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Azerbaijan's president says crashed jetliner was shot down by Russia unintentionally
item.description: Azerbaijan’s President Ilham Aliyev said Sunday that the Azerbaijani airliner that crashed last week was shot down by Russia, albeit unintentionally, and criticized Moscow for trying to “hush up” the issue for days. "We can say with complete clarity that the plane was shot down by Russia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China directs largest military build-up since 1930s Nazi Germany, expert warns, citing Pentagon report
item.description: Defense Department report reveals China's rapidly growing weapons arsenal and "psychological warfare" operation ahead of new Trump presidency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese man sentenced to death for killing 35 people after driving into a crowd
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A court in China has sentenced a man to death for killing 35 people last month by driving into a crowd, in an attack that raised national concern about mass killings.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, WGN 9
